title: Burn cooling and referral card
category: Medical and first · Aid · Disaster · Health
summary: Medical-heavy survival entry for cooling burns early, removing tight items, covering loosely, and escalating red flags. It is designed for offline emergencies and does not replace professional care.
content: Use this entry to organize cooling burns early, removing tight items, covering loosely, and escalating red flags. Start with scene safety, hand hygiene, calm communication, and written timing. Keep the person comfortable, protect privacy, avoid risky experiments, and prepare a clear handover for a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, or trained first-aider. Watch especially for: face, hands, genitals, large area, deep burn, chemical burn, electrical burn, child, or breathing trouble. In a long emergency, review the person regularly instead of assuming they are stable.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Burn cooling and referral card”.
soap or sanitizer if available — for «Burn cooling and referral card»
gloves or clean barrier if available — for «Burn cooling and referral card»
paper and pen. Context: “Burn cooling and referral card”.
phone or radio if available — for «Burn cooling and referral card»
labels for times and medicines — for «Burn cooling and referral card»
steps: Check that the place is safe for the helper and the injured or sick person. Apply it specifically within the “Burn cooling and referral card” procedure.
Call emergency help or send a reliable person for help when any red flag is present. Apply it specifically within the “Burn cooling and referral card” procedure.
Write the time the problem started, what changed, medicines taken, allergies, and known conditions. Apply it specifically within the “Burn cooling and referral card” procedure.
Use clean hands and clean barriers before touching wounds, body fluids, food, medicines, infants, or medical devices. Apply it specifically within the “Burn cooling and referral card” procedure.
Keep the person warm or cool as appropriate, still if trauma is possible, and protected from crowds. Apply it specifically within the “Burn cooling and referral card” procedure.
Recheck and record changes regularly so deterioration is noticed early. Apply it specifically within the “Burn cooling and referral card” procedure.
Prepare a short handover note with name, age, symptoms, times, medicines, allergies, and actions already taken. Apply it specifically within the “Burn cooling and referral card” procedure.
warnings: Do not use this as a replacement for training, emergency services, a clinician, or local medical guidance. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Burn cooling and referral card”.

Do not guess medicine doses, mix medicines without label checking, share prescriptions, or perform invasive procedures. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Burn cooling and referral card”.
Escalate urgently if you see: face, hands, genitals, large area, deep burn, chemical burn, electrical burn, child, or breathing trouble.
Keep children, elders, pregnancy, disability, immune weakness, and chronic illness in the high-risk group. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Burn cooling and referral card”.
common_mistakes: Waiting too long after red flags appear while carrying out “Burn cooling and referral card”
Not writing times and medicines while carrying out “Burn cooling and referral card”
Touching wounds or fluids without hand hygiene while carrying out “Burn cooling and referral card”
Letting crowds block air, privacy, or transport while carrying out “Burn cooling and referral card”
Assuming a quiet person is stable without rechecking while carrying out “Burn cooling and referral card”
alternatives: Use official first-aid training when available as a fallback for “Burn cooling and referral card”
Use local emergency numbers and pharmacists for medicine questions as a fallback for “Burn cooling and referral card”
Use paper handover notes when phones are dead as a fallback for “Burn cooling and referral card”
Use a trusted translator if language blocks care as a fallback for “Burn cooling and referral card”
failure_signs: Red flags appear: face, hands, genitals, large area, deep burn, chemical burn, electrical burn, child, or breathing trouble
The person becomes harder to wake, breathe, drink, speak, walk, or recognize during “Burn cooling and referral card”
Pain, bleeding, swelling, fever, confusion, or weakness gets worse during “Burn cooling and referral card”
when_not_to_use: Do not delay emergency care to complete the checklist when using the “Burn cooling and referral card” record
Do not move a trauma patient unless staying is more dangerous when using the “Burn cooling and referral card” record
Do not force care on a conscious person except immediate life safety situations under local rules when using the “Burn cooling and referral card” record
short_term: Stabilize the scene, reduce immediate harm, record key facts, and connect the person to trained help. This note applies specifically to Burn cooling and referral card.
long_term: Keep medical records updated, train the group in certified first aid, maintain supplies, and practice respectful handovers. This note applies specifically to Burn cooling and referral card.
if_method_fails: Escalate to emergency services, nearest clinic, pharmacist, radio medical consult, or evacuation route; document the failed step and time. This note applies specifically to Burn cooling and referral card.
environment_notes: In apartments, heat, smoke, poor ventilation, crowds, no elevator, and water outages can make medical problems worse. Plan quiet space, clean water, light, and transport. This note applies specifically to Burn cooling and referral card.

title: Chemical splash rinse station
category: Medical and first · Aid · Disaster · Health
summary: Medical-heavy survival entry for rapid rinsing, clothing removal boundary, label preservation, and emergency contact. It is designed for offline emergencies and does not replace professional care.
